_publications._publicationTitle: Ex vivo to in vivo model of malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors for precision oncology

_contextSnippet: We successfully assembled PDX into 3D microtissues, categorized as robust (>90% viability at 48 h), good (>50%), or unusable (<50%). We evaluated drug response to 'robust' or 'good' microtissues, namely MN-2, JH-2-002, JH-2-079-c, and WU-225. Drug response ex vivo predicted drug response in vivo.
details: PDX cells dissociated using Miltenyi human Tumor Dissociation Kit and GentleMACS; murine cells depleted using Mouse Cell Depletion Kit; cells encapsulated at 6×10^6 cells/mL in collagen I (6 mg/mL) + 10% Matrigel microtissues via microfluidic droplet generation; microtissues placed in agarose microwell plates; viability at 48 h assessed by Calcein AM (live) and DRAQ5 (nuclei) with Zeiss Axio Observer and Cellpose/ImageJ quantification; drug testing: trabectedin (chemotherapy), olaparib (PARP inhibitor), mirdametinib (MEK inhibitor) as single agents and combinations; ex vivo dose-response curves (8-point, 4-fold dilutions) generated in GraphPad Prism; ex vivo responses in JH-2-002 and WU-225 microtissues predicted matched in vivo PDX responses
